title: Complex Creamy Uruguayan Jasmine Rice Skillet
description: A dense and floral Uruguayan dish featuring jasmine rice, perfect for a post-workout meal, made in a cast-iron skillet with a Jain-friendly approach, kneading the rice for extra creaminess.
instructions: Heat the melted coconut oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the toasted cumin seeds and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the rinsed jasmine rice to the skillet, stirring to coat the rice in the oil and mix with the onion and garlic.
Knead the rice mixture in the skillet for about 5 minutes, until the rice is well coated and starting to break down.
Add the dried jasmine flowers, vegetable broth, and salt to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is creamy.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it rest, covered, for 5 minutes before serving.
